channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe Makes Historic Attempt to Circle the Sun
item.description: NASA's Parker Solar Probe makes historic attempt to circle the sun, flying closer than any spacecraft before, and shedding light on the sun's impact on Earth.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Navy confronting hurdles to implementing 5G-enabled technologies at scale
item.description: 5G makes exciting emerging technologies possible, but first the Department of the Navy has to solve spectrum integration, cybersecurity and acquisition hurdles. The post Navy confronting hurdles to implementing 5G-enabled technologies at scale first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Xerox buys Lexmark for $1.5 billion to build US printer giant capable of rivaling HP
item.description: Xerox is buying Lexmark in $1.5 billion deal which could build a printer giant capable of taking on HP.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: WinGet: The best way to keep Windows apps updated
item.description: For more than two years, I’ve been working with WinGet daily to monitor and maintain the apps on my Windows 10 and 11 PCs. For those not already in the know, WinGet is the built-in, command-line interface to Microsoft’s Windows Package Manager service.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Banning Chinese-made drones could hurt some Americans
item.description: Russell Hedrick, a North Carolina farmer, flies drones to spray fertilizers on his corn, soybean and wheat fields at a fraction of what it would cost him to use a conventional ground spreader.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk’s xAI supercomputer gets 150MW power boost despite concerns over grid impact and local power stability
item.description: xAI gains 150MW power approval for its supercomputer, but locals fear impact on grid and future power availability.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: A $100bn tech company you've probably never heard of is teaming up with the world's biggest memory manufacturers to produce supercharged HBM
item.description: $100bn tech company is teaming up with the world's biggest memory manufacturers to produce supercharged HBM.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Tech expert warns of device that disables Wi-Fi cameras
item.description: It just takes a flick of a switch, and it can make your Wi-Fi camera stop working and miss the crucial video you need.
item.dc:creator: Elena Cousino, NewsNation

item.title: Hallucinations to agents: Here’s how AI language models evolved in 2024
item.description: I research the intersection of artificial intelligence, natural language processing, and human reasoning as the director of the Advancing Human and Machine Reasoning lab at the University of South Florida.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Windows 11 suffers more bugs in latest update, with the Start menu hit hard by some frustrating issues
item.description: Windows 11’s December update introduces visual glitches, problems that are torpedoing the Start menu, and a sprinkling of other bugs.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The 4 key aspects of a successful data strategy
item.description: How do data strategies work and do companies even need them? This question is on the minds of many business leaders today. At the same time, they are under increasing pressure to remain competitive and innovative.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Requisitos que deben cumplir los líderes de TI para navegar en la era de disrupción actual
item.description: Para su Informe sobre la preparación para la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa, la compañía de servicios de TI Avanade encuestó a más de 3.000 ejecutivos de TI y empresas de 10 países con, al menos, 500 millones de dólares en ingresos anuales.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Overcoming modern observability challenges
item.description: Observability, at its core, is about collecting and analyzing data generated from applications, cloud computing resources, and edge devices to ensure software and services are run smoothly, user experiences are optimized, and resources are managed cost-effectively.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Top 5 mistakes that could expose your financial data to cybercriminals
item.description: There are common habits and oversights that could leave your financial information vulnerable to cybercriminals, according to Kurt the CyberGuy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe aims to fly closer to the sun like never before
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A NASA spacecraft aims to fly closer to the sun than any object sent before. The Parker Solar Probe was launched in 2018 to get a close-up look at the sun. Since then, it has flown straight through the sun's corona: the outer atmosphere visible during a total solar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Food tracking just got lazy — in the best way possible — with this wearable
item.description: Counting calories just got easier with The Drop, the world's first automated nutrition tracker using advanced computer vision and artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The future belongs to systems of action
item.description: The world of enterprise tech is built on sturdy foundations. For decades, systems of record—the databases, customer relationship management (CRM), and enterprise resource planning (ERP) platforms we’re all intimately familiar with—have served as reliable pillars underpinning every major business.
item.dc:creator: John Sabino, Fast Company

item.title: Ex-OpenAI engineer who raised legal concerns about the technology he helped build has died
item.description: Suchir Balaji, a former OpenAI engineer and whistleblower who helped train the artificial intelligence systems behind ChatGPT and later said he believed those practices violated copyright law, has died, according to his parents and San Francisco officials.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Andrew Ng is betting big on agentic AI
item.description: Andrew Ng came up with a theory almost 15 years ago: What if Google, the internet giant, thought of machines like the brain and trained neural networks on mass amounts of data using computational power in an attempt to lead to artificial general intelligence? Google bit on Ng’s line of thinking, an
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Arm’s high-stakes licensing suit against Qualcomm ends in mistrial, but Qualcomm prevails in key areas
item.description: Arm has lost a battle over licensing of its microprocessor designs to Qualcomm, ending doubt over the immediate future of some of the chip maker’s products.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Amazon workers are striking at multiple facilities. Here's what you should know
item.description: Amazon workers affiliated with the Teamsters union continued a strike Friday at seven of the company's delivery hubs just days before Christmas. At midnight on Saturday, workers at a prominent unionized warehouse in New York will also join, the Teamsters said.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tesla recalling almost 700K vehicles over tire pressure monitors
item.description: Tesla is recalling nearly 700,000 vehicles over an issue with the warning light on the tire pressure monitoring system.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: New iPhone auto-restart feature locks encrypted data after extended inactivity
item.description: Apple's iOS 18.1 Inactivity Reboot automatically reboots your iPhone if it hasn't been used or unlocked for more than three days, providing better data protection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scholz dismisses Musk's assertion that only a far-right party can 'save' Germany
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz on Friday dismissed an assertion by Elon Musk that only a far-right party can “save Germany,” but said that freedom of opinion "also goes for multibillionaires.” Germany is expected to vote in an early election on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Smart policing revolution: How Kazakhstan is setting a global benchmark
item.description: In the era of digital transformation, public safety stands at a critical crossroads. Law enforcement agencies globally are under increasing scrutiny to enhance transparency, efficiency, and trust within their communities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Amazon workers strike at multiple facilities as Teamsters seek labor contract
item.description: Workers at seven Amazon facilities went on strike Thursday, an effort by the Teamsters union to pressure the e-commerce company for a labor agreement during a key shopping period.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Australian who falsely claimed to have invented bitcoin is found in contempt of UK court
item.description: LONDON (AP) — An Australian computer scientist who falsely claimed to be the founder of the bitcoin cryptocurrency was found Thursday to be in contempt of an order of London's High Court and was sentenced to 12 months in prison, suspended for two years.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk considers funding Nigel Farage's populist party, a move that could shake up UK politics
item.description: LONDON (AP) — It’s a photo that sent a tremor through British politics: Elon Musk flanked by British politician Nigel Farage and a wealthy backer, in front of a gilt-framed painting of a young Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, ABC 4
